correlation.interpretation: Each 1°C rise in average temperature increases peak grid demand by 4.50 GW (95% CI: 3.79–5.22 GW). Grid enters critical stress (>260 GW) when temp exceeds 44.2°C.

forecasting.note: MAPE computed on 10-day held-out period only. 2026 peak pinned to Grid-India confirmed record.

recommendation.headline: Prioritise Heat Action Plan (HAP) investment in 5 Critical/Very High states: Odisha, Chhattisgarh, Jharkhand, Rajasthan, Madhya Pradesh — where 34% of national economic loss is concentrated in 12% of the population.
recommendation.investment_case: Under the Base Case scenario, unmitigated heat stress costs India $182B/year by 2030. A $1B targeted intervention in the top-5 states (cooling infrastructure, work-hour regulations, early warning systems) has an estimated 50:1 ROI against projected productivity losses in those states alone.
recommendation.policy_gap: Critical gap: Heatwaves are NOT classified as natural disasters under India's Disaster Management Act 2005 — limiting formal relief fund flows. Reclassification is the single highest-leverage policy action available.

assumptions_and_limitations.data_lag: 2026 granular daily station-level temperature data from IMD is not yet publicly available (IMD releases data with a 3-6 month lag). State-level figures use highest confirmed temperatures from official IMD press releases and verified media reports. Grid demand figures use confirmed Grid-India PSP reports.
assumptions_and_limitations.economic_allocation: State-level economic losses are allocated proportionally from national totals using outdoor workforce × population × heatwave days weighting. Direct state-level income surveys are not yet available for 2026.
assumptions_and_limitations.what_would_change: If granular IMD station data is released: heatwave-days count could shift ±2 days per state, potentially reordering Bihar/Jharkhand in risk tiers. If 2026 formal economic surveys show informal wage recovery > 40% baseline, the economic loss figures would compress by 10-15%.
assumptions_and_limitations.model_limitations: Polynomial degree-2 forecast achieves 0.6% MAPE on a 10-day held-out period. Short test window; a seasonal ARIMA model would improve long-range accuracy but requires statsmodels (not available in this env).
